channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Montana Senate race, which could determine majority, seeing 'intense ground game operation': NRSC Chairman
item.description: NRSC Chairman Steve Daines told Fox News Digital in an exclusive interview he is confident Republicans will secure a Senate majority by flipping the Montana Senate seat this cycle.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Massachusetts, Nevada, New Mexico launch early in-person voting
item.description: Early voting is underway in Massachusetts, Nevada and New Mexico with just 17 days to go until the general election on Nov. 5. Find out how to vote in this article.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: States to election officials: Follow the law or face consequences
item.description: In at least five battleground states, officials have investigated, indicted and even jailed officials who tried to interfere with the vote count.
item.dc:creator: Rich Johnson, NewsNation

item.title: Harris eyes a rural Maine congressional district in a hunt for every possible electoral vote
item.description: One of the largest, coldest and most rural congressional districts in the country could figure prominently in this year’s presidential election.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Whittle, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: A failed mic leaves Donald Trump pacing the stage in silence for nearly 20 minutes
item.description: DETROIT (AP) — Donald Trump paced his rally stage in silence for nearly 20 minutes Friday night in Detroit after his microphone cut out.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE L. PRICE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Poll finds majority of Americans say they're worse off than four years ago, similar to 1992 election
item.description: A Gallup poll suggested that the majority of Americans believe they are worse off than they were four years ago, citing economic strain as a top issue.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Home stretch: Harris, Trump hold dueling events, rallies in crucial battleground state
item.description: Vice President Kamala Harris and former President Trump held dueling campaign events in battleground Michigan with less than three weeks to go until Election Day.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: After Roberson ruling, upcoming Court of Criminal Appeals election in the spotlight
item.description: Three of the five judges who allowed Robert Roberson’s execution to proceed will be leaving the court after Paxton-backed primary challenges.
item.dc:creator: Texas Tribune, By Eleanor Klibanoff, KPRC 2

item.title: Texas Republicans demand answers about Venezuelan gang members
item.description: White House spokesperson Angelo Fernández Hernández has called reports Biden is "secretly flying" people into the U.S. “categorically false.
item.dc:creator: Katie Smith, NewsNation

item.title: British invasion: Labour Party to descend on US to help Harris
item.description: In a now-deleted social media post, the head of operations for the British Labour Party indicated "nearly 100" current and former party staffers will be headed to the United States to help Vice President Kamala Harris during the final stretch before the election.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Former Minnesota Congressman Rick Nolan dies at age 80
item.description: Former U.S. Rep. Rick Nolan, who represented two of Minnesota’s congressional districts in Washington more than 30 years apart, has died at the age of 80.
item.dc:creator: MPR News Staff and Dan Kraker, MPR News

item.title: Trump family will get 75% of crypto token revenue
item.description: The Trump family's cryptocurrency platform, World Liberty Financial, indicated in a report released Thursday that DT Marks DEFI LLC, a Delaware-based company connected to the former president, could get 75 percent of WLF's protocol revenues.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Nevada Senate hopefuls tackle trans athletes, immigration and UFOs in only debate
item.description: Sen. Jackie Rosen and Republican candidate Sam Brown went head to head in the Nevada Senate debate on immigration, the economy and biological males competing in women's sports.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: How Republicans mainstreamed the baseless idea of noncitizen voting in 2024
item.description: Donald Trump and his allies have zeroed in on the baseless claim that Democrats are encouraging newly arrived migrants to vote for them in the 2024 election. There is no evidence of a plot like this.
item.dc:creator: Jude Joffe-Block and Miles Parks, MPR News

item.title: SBA administrator visits Tampa Bay as disaster loan program runs dry
item.description: While visiting businesses in Tampa Bay, which included both of those businesses, SBA Administrator Isabel Casillas Guzman said the next move is up to members of Congress.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Almost 23% of people say politics influences where they live: Poll
item.description: "Some go so far as to consider moving to find that alignment," Danielle Hale, chief economist for Realtor.com, said.
item.dc:creator: Cassie Buchman, NewsNation

item.title: Early voting begins in North Carolina, officials helping after Helene
item.description: With the election less than three weeks away, many residents are still reeling from Helene. This has led to some drastic changes to the voting process.
item.dc:creator: Devan Markham, NewsNation

item.title: Harris Fox News interview draws sharp reactions on both sides
item.description: “If she wins, it will be because of that answer … which is ‘I’m not Joe Biden,’” columnist Chris Cillizza noted.
item.dc:creator: Rich Johnson, NewsNation

item.title: JD Vance says 'no,' he does not think Trump lost in 2020
item.description: Vance has faced repeated questions on the campaign trail about whether Trump lost in 2020. On Wednesday, he gave his most direct answer to date, answering "no" in response to a reporter's question.
item.dc:creator: Jason Breslow, MPR News

item.title: Harris interview on Fox gets testy — but also gives her a do-over
item.description: The interview was the vice president's first formal sit-down with Fox. She faced questions on immigration, and was given a chance to answer again what she would do differently from President Biden.
item.dc:creator: Tamara Keith, MPR News

item.title: Elon Musk commits $70 million to boost Donald Trump
item.description: Elon Musk plunged more than $70 million into helping Donald Trump and other Republicans win in November's election, making him one of the biggest donors to GOP causes this campaign season, according to campaign finance disclosures released this week.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN SLODYSKO, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Trump would be the oldest person to become president. He’s not sharing health details
item.description: If he wins next month’s election, Donald Trump would be the oldest person in U.S. history elected president. Yet the 78-year-old Republican nominee is refusing to disclose new details about his physical or mental well-being, breaking decades of precedent.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Nebraska court allows voter registrations from people with felony convictions
item.description: Nebraskans with felony convictions may register to vote after the state's highest court ruled Wednesday that a top election official had no authority to deem a law restoring those rights unconstitutional.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Control of the Minnesota House is up for grabs this fall. Here are 14 races to watch
item.description: While all seats in the Minnesota House of Representatives are on the ballot this fall, just a fraction are taking up the focus and much of the fundraising for the major parties.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, MPR News

item.title: Small business disaster loan program is out of money until new funds are approved
item.description: The SBA’s disaster loan program has run out of money, it announced on Tuesday. The agency expects to receive new funding from Congress, and will continue to accept applications in the meantime.
item.dc:creator: Andrea Hsu and Miguel Macias, MPR News

item.title: Minnesota city says Trump campaign still owes more than $200,000 for July rally
item.description: Local officials say former President Donald Trump’s campaign still owes a central Minnesota city nearly $209,000 for services related to a July campaign rally. The Minnesota Star Tribune reports that the bill is for services beyond what St. Cloud normally provides.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Nevada DMV fast-tracks credentials for potential voters
item.description: The Nevada Department of Motor Vehicles is seeking to move potential voters to the front of the line starting next week and is reminding new residents they can surrender their out-of-state ID.
item.dc:creator: James Schaeffer, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: NJ lawmakers' fight against teen vaping continues with new bill
item.description: TRENTON, N.J. (PIX11) -- The fight against youth vaping continues in the Garden State as some Trenton lawmakers are trying to pass a law prohibiting the delivery of electronic smoking devices to people under 21, requiring someone over 21 to sign off on the delivery.
item.dc:creator: Jim Vasil, PIX 11
